scraped_bill_text.title: American Rescue Plan Act of 2021
scraped_bill_text.text: AN ACT To provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of S. Con. Res. 5. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, SECTION 1. SHORT TITLE. This Act may be cited as the ``American Rescue Plan Act of 2021''. SEC. 9011. RESTAURANT REVITALIZATION GRANT PROGRAM. (a) In General.--The Secretary shall make grants to eligible entities in which the proceeds of such grants are used for allowable expenses, as provided in this section. (b) Eligible Entities.--An entity shall be eligible to receive a grant under this section if the entity-- (1) is an eligible entity as of March 11, 2020; (2) is not a publicly traded company; and (3) as of March 11, 2020, owns or operates 20 or fewer locations, regardless of whether those locations share the same name, and does so under the same name as the entity had on that date. (c) Amount Of Grants.--The amount of a grant to an eligible entity under this section shall be equal to the amount by which the pandemic-related revenue loss of the eligible entity exceeds the amount of the assistance described in subsection (g)(2)(B) received by the entity. (d) Pandemic-Related Revenue Loss.--For purposes of this section, the term ``pandemic-related revenue loss'' means, with respect to an eligible entity, the amount (not less than zero) which is equal to-- (1) the gross receipts of the eligible entity in calendar year 2019; minus (2) the gross receipts of the eligible entity in calendar year 2020. (e) Allowable Expenses.--An eligible entity may use grant amounts made available under this section only for the following expenses, which shall be verified by the entity in such manner as the Secretary may require: (1) Payroll. (2) Principal or interest on any mortgage obligation. (3) Rent, including rent under a lease agreement. (4) Utilities. (5) Supplies, including protective equipment and cleaning materials. (6) Food and beverage expenses that are within the scope of the normal business practice of the entity. (7) Covered supplier costs. (8) Operational expenses. (9) Paid sick leave. (10) Any other expenses that the Administrator determines are essential to maintaining the eligible entity. (f) Funding.--There is appropriated to the Secretary, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $28,600,000,000, to remain available until expended, for making grants under this section. In addition, there is appropriated to the Secretary, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $10,000,000,000, to remain available until expended, for making grants under this section to eligible entities described in subsection (b)(3)(A) that meet the additional requirements under paragraph (2) of such subsection.

rag_chunks.content: SEC. 9011. RESTAURANT REVITALIZATION GRANT PROGRAM. The Secretary shall make grants to eligible entities in which the proceeds of such grants are used for allowable expenses, as provided in this section. Funding appropriated: $28,600,000,000 for restaurant revitalization grants.

The amount of a grant to an eligible entity shall be equal to the pandemic-related revenue loss minus any assistance received. Pandemic-related revenue loss equals 2019 gross receipts minus 2020 gross receipts.

CBO Estimate: The American Rescue Plan Act is estimated to cost approximately $1.9 trillion over 10 years. Key provisions include direct payments, extended unemployment benefits, and small business aid.

web_sources.title: Estimated Budgetary Effects of the American Rescue Plan Act
web_sources.snippet: CBO estimates that enacting the American Rescue Plan Act would increase the deficit by $1.844 trillion over the 2021-2030 period.

web_sources.title: Fact Sheet: The American Rescue Plan Will Deliver Immediate Economic Relief
web_sources.snippet: The American Rescue Plan will change the course of the pandemic and deliver immediate economic relief to American families.
